thread.title: Trump taps Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Labor Secretary | outtherecolorado.com
thread.title_full: Trump taps Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Labor Secretary | outtherecolorado.com

author: Annabella Rosciglione, Washington Examiner
title: Trump taps Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Labor Secretary | outtherecolorado.com
text:
President-elect [Donald Trump](https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/tag/donald-trump/) nominated outgoing Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer (R-OR) to be secretary of the Department of Labor.
Chavez-DeRemer has been [described](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/us/elections/house-election-swing-districts.html) by the New York Times as a "a mainstream Republican" who pitches herself as an independent thinker. Trump, in his nomination, said her appointment “will achieve historic cooperation between Business and Labor that will restore the American Dream for Working Families.”
“Lori has worked tirelessly with both Business and Labor to build America’s workforce, and support the hardworking men and women of America,” a statement from Trump said.
[CLICK HERE TO READ MORE FROM THE WASHINGTON EXAMINER](http://washingtonexaminer.com/)
“I look forward to working with her to create tremendous opportunity for American Workers, to expand Training and Apprenticeships, to grow wages and improve working conditions, to bring back our Manufacturing jobs,” the statement continued.
Chavez-DeRemer, a one-term congresswoman, recently lost reelection to Rep.-elect Janelle Bynum (D-OR). Had Chavez-DeRemer won the race, Trump’s pick would have narrowed the Republican's [already thin House majority](https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/news/house/3230665/house-republicans-confront-math-problem-trump-erodes-majority/).
